rationale: Both candidates correctly validate the core codes.audio fix but miss GT's two specific non-blocking asks (update the stale docstring/module comment, and question whether the legacy top-level-audio producer still exists). X's two follow-up comments are tightly grounded, verified with a concrete failing example (short 'hello' vs 'hello world goodbye' passing containment), and closer in spirit to GT's concern about the fallback's scope/behavior. Y produces more findings but many are low-value nits (docstring for a different function, magic-number extraction, speculative dead-code claim) that dilute precision and don't better match GT's actual concerns despite the extensive validation sweep.
